ip_outcome_note: The flavor text explicitly names Kaya: 'Etrata evaded the Boros arresters and Azorius lawmages, but not even a master assassin could give Kaya the slip.' Kaya, Spirits' Justice (MKM no. 211) is a named legendary planeswalker in the same set — Wizards designed her as the only retained-spark planeswalker in MKM. The pursuer in the art carries a pink-violet ethereal orb consistent with Kaya's signature ghost-phasing / spirit-manipulation magic. The flavor text explicitly identifies the pursuer as Kaya (the non-fleeing figure who catches a master assassin). Combined: flavor text names Kaya as the pursuer, the art shows a pursuer with ghostly violet magic, and a Kaya planeswalker card exists in the same set. This meets the evidence bar: flavor text explicitly names the figure. ip_verified: true.

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: 'Etrata evaded the Boros arresters and Azorius lawmages, but not even a master assassin could give Kaya the slip.' [Scryfall] Kaya Cassir is a ghost-hunting planeswalker and former guildmaster of the Orzhov Syndicate on Ravnica. In MKM she works alongside Alquist Proft to solve the Karlov Manor murders. Etrata is a Dimir vampire assassin who attended the Karlov Manor party in disguise and was captured after an attempted escape. [WebSearch snippet: Murders at Karlov Manor lore summary]
sources: WebSearch snippet: MKM story Etrata Kaya

trivia_bullets.text: Kaya is the only planeswalker in MKM who retained her spark through the Phyrexian invasion's Desparkening. Her presence in the set is significant: she is explicitly named in multiple flavor texts across the set and has her own legendary planeswalker card (Kaya, Spirits' Justice, MKM no. 211). [WebSearch snippet: Draftsim MKM planeswalkers]
sources: WebSearch snippet: Kaya Spirits Justice MKM

trivia_bullets.text: Target creature gets +3/+0 and gains first strike until end of turn, plus Investigate (create a Clue token). Legal in Pauper (common), Standard, Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, Vintage, and Commander. The Investigate keyword ties it to MKM's broader detective-procedural mechanic suite — combat trick that also generates evidence. [Scryfall]
